229971.explanation: Municipality E-V must properly involve residents, but not through referenda. Referenda are very costly, time-consuming, and unsuited for complex issues with major consequences. VD|80 prefers good participation at an early stage—such as at De Lange Weeren—so that residents truly have influence.

229972.explanation: The 'so-called' right to challenge already exists. Volunteers and sports clubs can sometimes take over management of a sports hall or park with greater involvement. This must be weighed case by case and only used if it clearly adds value for quality, engagement, and continuity.

229973.explanation: VD|80 wants to lower the property tax (OZB) as soon as financial space allows, to relieve residents. This should not come at the expense of services and livability. Because municipalities receive more mandatory tasks from the national government, the national contribution must also be fairer.

229974.explanation: In many municipalities, the tourist tax has increased in recent years. For most visitors, this is no barrier to visiting our beautiful municipality (economic boost). This way, we have visitors contribute to services and livability without extra burden on residents' wallets.

229975.explanation: Fewer and fewer municipalities still levy dog tax. The original goal—combating stray dogs and rabies—is demonstrably outdated. Moreover, revenue is often not specifically spent on dog policies; this feels like money-grabbing. VD|80 is not in favor of this.

229976.explanation: VD|80 supports this statement, provided the mandatory task from the national government is abolished. We primarily build for our own residents. Due to the high share of owner-occupied homes and the limited number of social rentals, our municipality is not suitable for accommodating large numbers of status holders.

229977.explanation: There is no evidence of such necessity. Newcomers must adapt to our norms and values; additional integration expenditures are not desirable.

229978.explanation: There must always be room for custom solutions, and specific cases must also allow exceptions. Greenery contributes to livability and is therefore desirable in both urban and rural areas.

229979.explanation: Our concern here is especially about solar parks on agricultural land. They clutter the landscape. Small-scale initiatives for farmers' own power supply in rural areas should be exempt from this.

229980.explanation: Good education and broad development are top priorities for VD|80. For new plans, it is convenient if children can attend education and activities at one location—but this is not the most important. This is not always possible at existing schools. Sports facilities can also be outside school premises.

229981.explanation: A continuation of the ban is absolutely desirable. Coffeeshops do not fit in our municipality and increase the risk of nuisance and crime. They normalize usage and increase addiction risks (especially among youth). By preventing their establishment, we protect youth, livability, and safety.

229982.explanation: Extra camera surveillance at bus stops increases the safety of our residents, especially for young and elderly people, helps prevent vandalism and nuisance, and supports enforcement. Only where no cameras are installed yet, with a short retention period, strict access, and without unnecessary privacy invasions.

229983.explanation: In a community like Edam-Volendam, this goes too far. For issues as seen in Amsterdam this may be necessary, but here it has (so far) not proven necessary.

229984.explanation: This option is acceptable to us, provided financial resources are made available from the national government. Research shows that the municipality can primarily better actively manage housing affordability.

229985.explanation: There is no reason in our municipality, where there is scarcity in all housing categories (and especially great scarcity of affordable home ownership), to impose such a high requirement for adding social rental homes. The statutory target of 30% is already substantial.

229986.explanation: Six stories is, according to Volendam|80, the maximum for the town centers. Only where this fits in the environment are some height accents acceptable (for example at the redevelopment site of the Julianaweg industrial area). The desirability must be assessed case by case.

229987.explanation: In our municipality, this is already the case for the vast majority. Additional regulation through, for example, an owner-occupancy obligation and/or purchase protection is therefore not immediately necessary according to Volendam|80.

229988.explanation: There is a large housing shortage in the municipality of Edam-Volendam. There is a need for all housing categories. Adding new construction outside the built-up area, such as in the Lange Weeren, is more than desirable according to Volendam|80.

229990.explanation: There are already various initiatives, both from volunteers and the municipality, to support residents with lower incomes. Volendam|80 has not seen the necessity to expand these.

229991.explanation: For many parts of the built-up area in our municipality, a 30 km/h speed limit already applies. Volendam80 therefore does not see the need to reduce the speed, especially on paved roads in our municipality, to 30 km/h.

229992.explanation: Volendam|80 is of the opinion that this goes beyond the duty of care of a public body (a municipality); moreover, we believe this is logistically virtually impossible.

229993.explanation: The municipality has already established a complaints procedure; Volendam|80 therefore does not see the necessity for an ombudsman. Furthermore, appointing an ombudsman is a heavy measure for daily complaints regarding municipal work and/or communication.

229994.explanation: At present, the municipality spends a lot of financial resources on making the current administrative housing sustainable and maintaining it (4 buildings). A new town hall on the Tase site, combined with housing, is therefore the best use of the vacant land.

229995.explanation: Volendam|80 believes that human scale should be taken into account in correspondence to citizens. AI (artificial intelligence) may and can be used for this, but there must always be a human check to verify accuracy.

229997.explanation: It is not possible to allow anything that safety authorities have classified as unsafe. Such a construction has so far not been permitted by the mayor due to safety considerations.

229998.explanation: The new industrial area must be accessible to all entrepreneurs, and we do not want to give priority to any specific category. Volendam80 believes this should not be a political choice but that we should let the market do its work.

229999.explanation: Volendam|80 believes that at this moment there are no (serious) signals of nuisance or disturbance from these tours. It is therefore premature to implement such drastic measures.

230000.explanation: Volendam|80 believes that “neighborhood batteries” should come from resident initiatives. Therefore, allocating or reserving financial resources for neighborhood batteries is not a priority for Volendam80. Providing information from the municipality about the (im)possibilities is desirable.

230001.explanation: These types of subsidies are arranged nationally and fall under the responsibilities of the national government. We find it unwise for the municipality to grant subsidies for this itself, especially as it is difficult to find coverage for it. However, providing information from the municipality about national subsidies is appropriate.

230002.explanation: There are already national reporting points where abuses can be reported. Moreover, the absolute number of labor migrants in our municipality is low. We therefore do not see the necessity to allocate resources for this.

230003.explanation: Our municipality already has many off-leash areas for dogs, and we have not received signals that this needs to be (drastically) adjusted or increased.

230004.explanation: We are in the middle of the process regarding a new waste policy, in which the citizens’ opinions must still be incorporated via participation. We are NOT in favor of a variant of Pay-As-You-Throw at this stage.

230005.explanation: All entrepreneurs should have the same opportunities, and Volendam80 views forms of (positive) discrimination as undesirable.

230006.explanation: This question implies that the current situation is undesirable. Moreover, it concerns farmers' own land, on which they basically have the right to do what they want.

230007.explanation: VD|80 believes this is not part of the municipality’s responsibilities; tax money should not be spent on modifications for more greenery in residents’ gardens. Residents should primarily decide for themselves how to design their gardens (within the limits of the zoning plan).

230008.explanation: Volendam80 is of the opinion that there are enough recreational spots to carry out these activities. Investment was recently made in the existing skate and roller rink park.

230009.explanation: Institutions that contribute to maintaining the cultural-historical identity of our municipality should receive additional financial resources if budget allows. VD|80 considers the (volunteer) work for museums very valuable.

230010.explanation: Volendam|80 believes that such initiatives involving (positive) discrimination are undesirable. Furthermore, it is difficult to link a (financial) threshold to this, and we have not heard that there is a strong desire for this.

230011.explanation: If the situation requires it (and there is cause), Volendam80 believes the police may conduct preventive frisking to ensure the safety of our residents in entertainment areas.

230012.explanation: Such initiatives already exist where women can participate. VD|80 is not aware of an additional demand for such courses in our municipality. The problem of violence against women should be addressed at the root.

230013.explanation: There are already sufficient national campaigns raising awareness about digital fraud. Volendam80 therefore does not see the need for an additional local campaign.

230014.explanation: We are proponents and drivers of buying land ourselves (active land policy). That way, we keep control over costs and the composition of residential neighborhoods.

230015.explanation: The requirements currently set for housing construction are already high and expensive. Even stricter requirements are undesirable.

230016.explanation: We are already doing this, so let's continue. Moreover, the national government also imposes requirements in this regard.

230017.explanation: We are already doing this, so let's continue.

230018.explanation: There are national arrangements used on this subject. We receive no signals from society that there is great demand for this.

230019.explanation: People with low incomes are supported (also from the national government, such as with allowances). Furthermore, sufficient debt counseling is available in our municipality to arrange solutions. We have not received signals that these arrangements are insufficient.

230020.explanation: Books are free here and the parental contribution is voluntary.

230021.explanation: The 60+ bus here also operates with volunteers.

230022.explanation: More signs only lead to clutter. Only in places where there is a genuine lack is this desirable for safety reasons.

230023.explanation: Preferably for our own residents as much as possible; visitors may certainly contribute to our facilities (it works both ways when you visit somewhere). The primary question is whether there is any real use and necessity. If not, paid parking should not be used as a cash cow. As long as use and necessity are not proven, we oppose paid parking.

230024.explanation: The construction of such a garage costs a lot of money. This must gradually be paid back.

230025.explanation: Of course, with attention to the safety of cyclists, pedestrians, and other road users.

230026.explanation: But it is even stronger to do this collectively from Zaanstreek-Waterland, as is already happening now.

230027.explanation: We will support every initiative submitted in this area (provided it meets the conditions, of course).

230030.explanation: The municipality cannot finance the hospice itself, but can facilitate it. Preparation costs can be covered by the municipality.

230031.explanation: The municipality is not an employer of informal caregivers. However, we do believe that informal caregivers should be supported as much as possible, and this is already happening (also through subsidies).

230033.explanation: Good idea; this way the interests of the entire community become clear and can be weighed accordingly.

229971.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA likes to involve all residents of our municipality in decisions that are made. This can be done through citizen panels or meetings. This leads to good decisions. We do not consider a referendum a good way to involve residents, as it only allows for a yes/no question.

229972.explanation: Residents have many ideas and energy to make their municipality more beautiful and better. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to give these initiatives plenty of room. The municipality supports plans from society and gladly works together on management and implementation, with clear agreements about quality.

229973.explanation: A healthy budget requires smart choices. We choose policies that allow us to carry out many plans. If we lower the property tax, it means cuts have to be made. We believe the property tax cannot simply be increased, but we want responsible financial policy.

229974.explanation: With a higher tourist tax, visitors contribute to the facilities of our municipality. This keeps the contribution of residents limited and allows us to invest fairly in our municipality.

229975.explanation: The abolition of the dog tax has worked out well. Since then, there has been much clarity and calm. We will continue to focus on good facilities for dogs and their owners.

229976.explanation: As a municipality, we have the task of arranging shelter for status holders. These people also deserve good homes and a place in society. By realizing more social rental housing, we ensure more capacity, quality, and good integration.

229977.explanation: Status holders must be able to fully participate in our community. This requires good integration, such as learning the language. The municipality guides status holders in their search for suitable work. This can be done by connecting them with local employers. Together we build a strong community.

229978.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA chooses for a green municipality. We will plant 2,000 extra native trees and ensure that every felled tree is replaced, preferably nearby. In addition, we want the municipality to cut down trees less quickly.

229979.explanation: Smart use of space yields much. Therefore, GroenLinks-PvdA supports dual use of space, such as solar panels on roofs and above parking spaces. They also offer opportunities on lands without other functions, for example because they are polluted.

229980.explanation: Education and daycare in one place works pleasantly and clearly. Children learn, play, and relax in a safe environment at one trusted location.

229981.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA is not fundamentally against the use or legalization of soft drugs. Establishing coffee shops in our municipality can bring problems, for example nuisance in the immediate vicinity. Therefore, we prefer no coffee shops in Edam-Volendam.

229982.explanation: In places where residents feel unsafe, we invest specifically in camera surveillance. We do this together with residents, in locations where it really helps.

229983.explanation: The community enforcement officers (boa's) do valuable work. However, they are not trained for situations with a high risk of violence. In such situations, the police must take action. This way, everyone's role and responsibility remain clear. GroenLinks-PvdA does not see a baton for boa's as a solution.

229984.explanation: Buying a home is difficult for first-time buyers. They often cannot borrow enough money from the bank. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to help these young people. With a small second loan from the municipality, starters can still get a home. This way, the municipality helps our youth to have their own (t)home.

229985.explanation: Our municipality has far too few social rental homes. We believe a catch-up must be made. We do this by increasing the percentage of social rental housing in new construction projects from 30% to 40%. This way, we build enough rental homes for starters and the elderly.

229986.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to build higher so we can quickly build more homes. Buildings of more than six floors do not fit everywhere, but we want to look carefully at where this is possible. An example of such a place is the Julianaweg industrial area. By building higher, we help our youth find housing.

229987.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports a self-occupancy obligation for new homes. Houses are meant to be lived in, not to be resold for profit. With a self-occupancy obligation, we ensure that houses remain available for our residents.

229988.explanation: The landscape is valuable. Therefore, GroenLinks-PvdA wants to preserve the landscape of our municipality. We mainly build within existing centers and handle open space carefully. We absolutely do not want more construction in the Purmer next to the business park.

229990.explanation: People living in poverty deserve a dignified existence. We want to grant people with an income up to 130% of the social minimum access to poverty schemes. Now this limit is 110%. The municipality must help based on trust and provide support that truly helps.

229991.explanation: Traffic safety in built-up areas must improve. Cyclists and pedestrians must be able to move safely. Lowering the maximum speed in built-up areas to 30 km/h creates fewer dangerous situations, especially when crossing without traffic lights.

229992.explanation: Women living in poverty sometimes are forced to cut back on tampons and sanitary pads. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to help these women obtain free menstrual products in an easy and anonymous way. The municipality can set this up and execute it together with schools, community centers, and sports clubs.

229993.explanation: We take complaints seriously. An ombudsman handles complaints from residents about the municipality and can conduct unsolicited investigations. This person works independently from the municipality. GroenLinks-PvdA considers this a good way to improve municipal services.

229994.explanation: Civil servants deserve a modern and sustainable workplace. A new town hall with 62 homes on the Tase site offers space for working and living. It is also cheaper than renovating the current town hall.

229995.explanation: Artificial intelligence on the municipal website is a good tool to quickly find the information you seek. Artificial intelligence should never replace contact with civil servants. Also, the technology must first be properly tested to prevent incorrect answers.

229997.explanation: Tradition is very important to GroenLinks-PvdA, but safety comes first. The stalls at the Volendam fair were too risky, especially because of their construction. If stalls can be placed that meet safety requirements, then we would allow the stalls to return.

229998.explanation: The business park in the Purmer will be realized. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to utilize it for local, existing companies. This way, space is freed up elsewhere for housing. Reserving land for new companies means less land becomes available for housing construction.

229999.explanation: Tourists remain welcome. But residents increasingly suffer from groups of tourists on guided tours. These groups block streets, sidewalks, and passages. GroenLinks-PvdA wants smaller tour groups and active enforcement against groups that are too large.

230000.explanation: Neighborhood batteries offer opportunities for overloaded power grids. In suitable locations, we explore this smart solution.

230001.explanation: Sustainable heating must be possible for everyone. A heat pump is a considerable expense for most people, and not everyone has that money. The municipality should help these people with subsidies. Residents of neighborhoods that must be gas-free first receive priority access to the subsidy.

230002.explanation: Exploitation does not belong in our municipality. Fortunately, there are currently no signs that such situations are occurring. However, the municipality must remain alert and intervene quickly at any signs.

230003.explanation: Dogs are part of many households. More off-leash areas create happy dogs and owners. Therefore, we will set up more of these areas. Preferably with a fence or a natural barrier around them. This way, dogs can run and play without worries.

230004.explanation: Our waste costs are too high due to poor waste separation. Residents who separate waste properly now pay for the (high) costs of those who do not. This is unfair. Paying for your own residual waste rewards residents who separate well with lower waste costs than those who separate poorly.

230005.explanation: The development of the business park in Purmer is certain. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to make the business park as sustainable as possible. We want to encourage entrepreneurs to work more sustainably. A priority scheme for sustainable companies strengthens our local economy and makes our municipality greener.

230006.explanation: Our nature is in poor condition. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to restore nature. We are working with our farmers on nature development, for example by providing subsidies for flower-rich field edges. We also want to create more new nature together. More nature benefits both farmers and biodiversity.

230007.explanation: Green gardens contribute to more biodiversity and help against water nuisance. The municipality supports initiatives and expands subsidies where needed.

230008.explanation: Roller skating and skateboarding are popular among children and youth. When redesigning parks and playgrounds, the municipality should consider creating skate parks. For example, this could be possible in Boelenspark in Volendam.

230009.explanation: Our wonderful museums teach residents and visitors more about the history of Edam-Volendam. We want to invest more in our museums so collections are preserved and more exhibitions can take place. We also want to give our museums more security with fixed multi-year subsidies.

230010.explanation: People living in poverty should be able to fully participate in society. Sports and culture are part of that. With a municipal pass, these residents can attend these activities for free. This pass should be designed so that not everyone can see that the user has a low income.

230011.explanation: The police may only conduct preventive searches if there are clear safety risks. The nightlife area in Volendam is not unsafe enough to allow the police to search people preventively for prohibited weapons or substances. Therefore, GroenLinks-PvdA sees no reason to apply this.

230012.explanation: Our streets are for everyone. Unfortunately, women often still feel unsafe on the streets. We find this unacceptable. The municipality must start collaborations with organizations that offer self-defense courses for women. If necessary, the municipality should allocate funding for this.

230013.explanation: Digital scams are happening more often. We want to make our residents alert to this so criminals have no chance. The municipality must provide more information, for example by sharing stories from residents who have been scammed. This way we work together on our resilience.

230014.explanation: The municipality must do more to build affordable housing. Sometimes it is necessary to purchase land or buildings, for example if the owner does not want to (quickly) build. By buying land in our centers we create opportunities for housing without sacrificing open landscapes.

230015.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants 20% of new homes to be built using wood (timber frame) construction. Wood produces less emissions, is good to recycle, and speeds up housing construction.

230016.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports mixed neighborhoods. In these neighborhoods, people of different ages, income levels, and backgrounds live together. This creates contact and understanding between groups. Mixed neighborhoods foster more connection because groups interact.

230017.explanation: The municipality provides subsidies to insulate owner-occupied homes. We believe this subsidy should be increased to €2,000 per home. The current amount of €1,550 is often too low to cover insulation measures. Homeowners should also receive more support with insulating.

230018.explanation: People with disabilities should be able to fully participate in society. Paid work gives people dignity and independence. The municipality can help employers who hire people with disabilities with subsidies, for example to arrange proper guidance.

230019.explanation: People with debts deserve our support. The municipality must help residents with heavy debts get their lives back on track. Debt settlement is an option when nothing else works. However, we first want to look at less severe measures, such as a payment arrangement.

230020.explanation: Every child should be able to go to school without worries. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to help families in poverty by providing school supplies. This prevents children from feeling ashamed or being bullied because their parents have little money.

230021.explanation: The community bus is important for the accessibility of Zeevang. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to explore whether community buses could also run to Edam and Volendam, so residents of Zeevang can more easily reach the larger centers. If necessary, the municipality should subsidize this.

230022.explanation: Motorists who speed make our municipality less safe for everyone. GroenLinks-PvdA wants more action against speeding. We support speed-reducing measures such as road narrowing, traffic campaigns, and increased enforcement. Installing additional (digital) signs fits well here.

230023.explanation: There is too much car traffic in the centers of Edam and Volendam. This reduces livability. With paid parking we want to encourage motorists to use a bike or park outside the centers. Residents who do not live in the centers may park for free for one and a half hours.

230024.explanation: Day visitors to Volendam currently do not pay parking fees. By directing visitors to parking garages and charging parking fees, the municipality can invest more in the livability of the center. Residents not living in adjacent neighborhoods may park for free for one and a half hours.

230025.explanation: Accessibility for car traffic remains important. Not everyone can walk or bike, for example due to age. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to give cyclists and pedestrians more space compared to cars, but that does not mean making the center completely inaccessible to cars.

230026.explanation: There are long traffic jams on the N247 during rush hour. The lane narrowing near Broek in Waterland is a major bottleneck. GroenLinks-PvdA supports the tunnel plans and urges the province to finally start construction. The situation has lasted long enough. Time for action.

230027.explanation: The municipality’s finances are in order, but we must make tough choices. Not everything can happen at once. Setting up a shuttle service for tourists is not a priority for GroenLinks-PvdA. If an entrepreneur wants to initiate this, we are happy to discuss it.

230028.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to encourage residents to bike more often. Therefore, we want to improve safety for cyclists. Building bike paths separated from the car lanes is a good way to do this. Separating fast and slow traffic reduces the risk of accidents.

230030.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports good and accessible care. Everyone should be able to spend their final life phase in a familiar environment, close to friends and family. We therefore support the search for a hospice location. The municipality may also invest in this where needed.

230031.explanation: Caring for others besides your job and family life is hard work. Those who do this deserve all the support and appreciation from the municipality. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to enable caregivers to be relieved three days per month. This allows them to catch their breath.

230033.explanation: The opinions of children (and youth) are not heard enough. GroenLinks-PvdA believes appointing a child mayor is a good way to give children more influence. The child mayor can, for example, represent all children by contributing ideas on new play and sports policies.

229971.explanation: The Participation Act is still in its early stages and its implementation is still developing. Therefore, we currently see the ultimate form of participation through a referendum as too complex.

229972.explanation: Within the Participation Act there is recently a right to challenge. If you think something can be done better, cheaper, or more beautifully, with financial resources and guidance from the municipality, then the municipality must facilitate that.

229973.explanation: We want to keep the tax burden as low as possible for our residents.

229974.explanation: We are a tourist hotspot; visitors contribute to our general costs.

229975.explanation: Dog tax is an outdated levy and no longer serves the purpose it was once intended for. This was abolished on the initiative of Zeevangs Belang.

229976.explanation: We want to shorten the waiting time for social housing for our own residents to improve flow. To achieve this, priority should be applied as little as possible.

229977.explanation: We believe that the national regulations for benefits are sufficient now. So no more money needs to be allocated.

229978.explanation: For every tree felled in the municipality, a new one should be planted, but we want more. We want more consideration given to the loss of green volume. This can be done by planting larger trees or more trees. So one felled, ten planted.

229979.explanation: Zeevangs Belang does not believe in solar fields or gigantic wind farms on or around our municipality. We believe in small-scale affordable energy systems.

229980.explanation: It is easier for parents if everything is located at one site. We want educational institutions to be able to decide about this themselves.

229981.explanation: We have an anti-drugs policy. Facilitating a coffeeshop does not fit within this.

229982.explanation: Well-lit and spacious bicycle parking facilities encourage more use of public transport. For safety, camera surveillance needs to be installed here. This is for a safer feeling and to prevent theft and vandalism. This applies especially to the bus station in Edam.

229983.explanation: We believe the municipality must be a safe environment. If our community officers need support in their tasks, we must facilitate this.

229984.explanation: Young people have the right to affordable housing in our municipality. They have the right to their own place. The municipality can contribute through a starter loan. In addition, we want to build affordable starter and youth homes.

229985.explanation: We support a good mix of social rental and owner-occupied homes. Currently, starters in our municipality have no chance in the rental market. More rental homes are necessary.

229986.explanation: We should no longer shy away from high-rise buildings with more than 6 floors in the urban area of Volendam, provided there are good public transport connections.

229987.explanation: To prevent the purchase of homes by investors, an owner-occupancy obligation is necessary.

229988.explanation: We advocate preserving the characteristic character of both the urban and natural landscapes. The open peat meadow area with its ribbon villages is a piece of cultural heritage.

229990.explanation: Especially for people with low incomes, we see the necessity of facilitating basic necessities.

229991.explanation: If we want to offer more space and safety to pedestrians and cyclists, then certain areas should be designated as 30 km/h zones.

229992.explanation: Especially for people with low incomes, we see the necessity of facilitating basic necessities. This also includes menstrual products.

229993.explanation: There is a very good independent National Ombudsman who handles complaints from citizens about improper government conduct.

229994.explanation: For our services, we need good housing for our civil servants. Currently, they are spread over four locations. Our preference is for one new location on the Tase site.

229995.explanation: The "BuitenBeter" app is a good example here. In addition, the municipality should have an easily accessible website where common questions can be answered by AI.

229997.explanation: This is very unwise for safety reasons. The makeshift work causes unsafe situations.

229998.explanation: The business park is intended for relocating companies from the centers. This allows us to build homes on these infill locations.

229999.explanation: Volendam must remain attractive to visitors but above all liveable for its residents. To reduce pressure on the historic center and increase liveability, it is necessary to introduce stricter rules for tourist guided tours.

230000.explanation: We believe in small-scale affordable energy systems, such as wind and solar. Neighborhood batteries can be a good solution for storing jointly generated energy.

230001.explanation: Many houses in our municipality date from the previous century, are poorly insulated and difficult to heat with a heat pump. The costs of the energy transition can be too high for individual citizens and are not feasible without assistance.

230002.explanation: All our residents must be able to live and work here safely, including our labor migrants.

230003.explanation: Playgrounds are not intended for walking dogs. To prevent nuisance here, separate off-leash dog areas are indispensable.

230004.explanation: We want the polluter to pay. Not everything in one lump sum, but paying for the residual waste offered that must be incinerated.

230005.explanation: A small part of the Purmer is now designated for industrial activities for companies from Edam-Volendam. Their relocation can make them more sustainable and free up space for housing construction.

230006.explanation: Our farmers are the best managers of the rural area. Therefore, they should get sufficient time and financial support to achieve this.

230007.explanation: People should be encouraged to make their gardens greener.

230008.explanation: We must invest in places where young people can spend their free time, the so-called Youth Meeting Places (JOP). Such as a skate park or basketball court.

230009.explanation: Currently, we spend less on culture than the national average. That must grow. There should be a balanced distribution between the sports budget and culture budget.

230010.explanation: The sports policy pays attention to vulnerable target groups for whom sport is an important means for mental and physical well-being. A pass makes sports more accessible to a broader group of people, increases involvement, and serves various needs.

230011.explanation: We support an anti-drugs policy; therefore, more preventive searches should be conducted in entertainment areas to enhance safety.

230012.explanation: This will contribute to the feeling of safety for women.

230013.explanation: Digitization is making a big leap, which also brings risks that we must make our residents aware of. We want more informational evenings about safety.

230014.explanation: This is not only a municipal problem, but it does require an active attitude from our municipality. This can be done through a strategic land policy. Purmer West for the development of an industrial estate is a good example of this.

230015.explanation: The use of reused materials and bio-based building materials must be further encouraged. This is everyone's responsibility.

230016.explanation: We support a good mix of social rental and owner-occupied homes.

230017.explanation: Many houses in our municipality are poorly insulated and difficult to heat. The costs of the energy transition can be too high for individual citizens and are not feasible without assistance.

230018.explanation: People with a distance to the labor market can thus have a better chance of finding a workplace.

230019.explanation: Creditors receive one joint offer. This provides overview and structure.

230020.explanation: For people with low incomes, we see the necessity of facilitating basic necessities. We think of necessary school supplies in this context.

230021.explanation: With the efforts of many volunteers, the neighborhood bus plays an important role in Zeevang. It is the only connection between the small centers and Purmerend. An extension to Edam would be welcome.

230022.explanation: In the interest of traffic safety, speed-reducing measures are necessary in all centers. Social control by local residents can help with this. Where physical and visual solutions do not work, Smileys can be placed.

230023.explanation: The main source of income is the property tax (OZB). This can remain low if we introduce paid parking. That is possible if we tackle parking pressure in the tourist centers in a smart way. To spare our own residents, we choose paid parking for tourists and visitors.

230024.explanation: The main source of income is the property tax (OZB). This can remain low if we introduce paid parking. That is possible if we tackle parking pressure in the tourist centers in a smart way. To spare our own residents, we choose paid parking for tourists and visitors.

230025.explanation: Volendam must remain attractive for visitors, but above all livable for its residents. To reduce the pressure on the historic center and increase livability, it is necessary to keep tourist traffic out of the center.

230026.explanation: The N247 is an important access road that often has traffic jams. Solving this bottleneck is a priority.

230027.explanation: We are in favor of a shuttle service. This reduces tourist traffic in the center and improves the livability for residents.

230028.explanation: Safe walking and cycling routes are necessary for safety in the municipality.

230030.explanation: A hospice is a welcome addition to our care offerings. However, this is not a primary task of the municipality. The municipality should not look for a location. If an application comes in, the municipality should look at it favorably.

230031.explanation: Supporting informal caregivers is a high priority for us. If we expect more self-reliance from residents, then you need to facilitate that.

230033.explanation: Young people deserve a voice that counts. That is why we find it important that all ages are involved in public administration.

229971.explanation: We choose active resident participation, village/neighborhood visions, and clear communication; referendums are not our focus.

229972.explanation: The CDA supports room for own initiatives from residents, institutions, companies, and neighborhood participation in the management of green spaces and facilities.

229973.explanation: The CDA keeps the property tax (OZB) as low as possible; reducing burdens where responsible is our principle.

229974.explanation: We levy accommodation/tourist tax fairly and cost-effectively; reduction is not an end in itself.

229975.explanation: The CDA focuses on fair, cost-covering levies; dog tax does not apply in this context.

229976.explanation: The CDA believes we must seek room within the provincial task assignment to facilitate this.

229977.explanation: According to the CDA, we already invest in integration through language, learning, work, and associations; quality over quantity and aligned with the carrying capacity of facilities.

229978.explanation: We green neighborhoods and stimulate community gardens and parks.

229979.explanation: We protect high-quality agricultural land and support sustainable energy with local support, but not on agricultural land or business parks. No solar fields, especially since there are still many unused roofs.

229980.explanation: The CDA wants to continue investing in integral child centers and continuous development and learning lines. This is how the new schools are already being set up.

229981.explanation: The CDA is against coffeeshops or shisha lounges in our municipality.

229982.explanation: The CDA finds that camera surveillance can be part of security measures at central places and cycling routes.

229983.explanation: The CDA believes violence is the ultimate enforcement tool and must remain reserved for the police. However, our enforcers must radiate authority and be able to defend themselves where necessary for safety reasons.

229984.explanation: According to the CDA, starter loans weigh heavily on the budget. This money could be better used to build more homes so that more people are helped.

229985.explanation: The CDA believes housing construction is expensive. With more than 30% social rental housing (the national standard), there is a great risk that plans are not financially feasible. The distribution in new construction is fixed as a percentage in social rental and owner-occupied homes, mid-rent and owner-occupied, and free sector.

229986.explanation: We focus on livable, appropriate construction (courtyards, life-course resistant) and quality of the living environment. If these apartment buildings fit within the surroundings, we are open to them.

229987.explanation: The CDA believes residents deserve as many available homes as possible.

229988.explanation: We build where necessary, including 'an extra street' in small villages and development in Purmer and the Lange Weeren. The CDA is against a generic ban outside the built-up area.

229990.explanation: The CDA believes there should be a single desk, simpler arrangements, with a facilities guide and customization to combat poverty.

229991.explanation: We want 30 km/h in residential areas and 50 km/h on through roads; lowering speed in residential areas yes, but not on main roads. Residential areas can already jointly apply for this, by the way.

229992.explanation: The CDA believes we already deploy broadly on poverty reduction and equal opportunities.

229993.explanation: The CDA chooses proximity government, understandable communication, and less administrative burden.

229994.explanation: The CDA supports a new city hall with a parking hub on the Tase site. However, a conclusive business case is always the most important precondition. In relation to the regulations regarding noise contours, this is the best solution to also realize more than 60 homes on this site.

229995.explanation: The CDA finds this fine for simple questions and continues to focus on clear communication and better service.

229997.explanation: The CDA supports traditions and events. Safety is a high priority, but only if the hospitality industry can guarantee the safety fitting for an event of this size.

229998.explanation: The CDA finds reserving 30% too much. When companies relocate, space is created in the places they leave behind. Moreover, new construction is often too expensive for a starting company. Besides, there is enough space.

229999.explanation: We focus on quality tourism, with a healthy distribution and balance. However, enforcement will be logistically difficult to implement.

230000.explanation: The municipality should not invest in the power supply of individuals but should enable it through permits.

230001.explanation: The CDA believes we should not give residents money, but provide information and advice and possibly organize collective purchasing.

230002.explanation: Exploitation must always be combated! However, it must be established that this exploitation is present in our municipality. Furthermore, one must question whether investing money will solve this.

230003.explanation: The CDA believes that if possible, off-leash areas should be set up spread over our municipality. The principle must remain that dogs are leashed.

230004.explanation: The CDA is of the opinion that the polluter should pay, with strict control at the gate and stimulating/enforcing waste separation.

230005.explanation: The CDA believes that there are already high sustainability requirements for new establishments by default, so no additional priority needs to be given.

230006.explanation: Nature improvement and landscape management are the responsibility of the province; the municipality does not have authority over this.

230007.explanation: We green the environment and encourage neighborhood participation to achieve this. Bribing residents does not fit within this approach.

230008.explanation: The CDA wants an Urban Sports Park included in the renovation of Boelenspark.

230009.explanation: We strengthen art and culture and improve accessibility of museums. Giving money without a specific purpose does not fit here.

230010.explanation: There is already an existing scheme for low-income residents, through which we encourage accessible sports and culture.

230011.explanation: We must follow national regulations here; we focus on zero tolerance for antisocial behavior, more visible enforcement, and cameras.

230012.explanation: The CDA wants to encourage existing and emerging initiatives. We combat (sexual) transgressive behavior through prevention, training, and enforcement.

230013.explanation: The CDA believes we should organize informational evenings and campaigns about digital safety.

230014.explanation: We aim for realistic and affordable housing development plans and acceleration where possible. It can be strategic for the municipality to purchase land, but this is not an end in itself.

230015.explanation: We build sustainably and future-proof. The CDA has already stated in 2021 that it wants to stimulate 20% timber construction in new buildings. However, it must remain affordable.

230016.explanation: We focus on mixed neighborhoods with a blend of social rental, mid-rent/ownership, and private sector housing.

230017.explanation: There are existing subsidy schemes that we want to continue deploying and support with energy coaches.

230018.explanation: We support inclusive employers and the jobs agreement. When people are inactive at home, they cost society money anyway.

230019.explanation: We want to investigate whether the municipality can take over all debts so that there is only one creditor. Buying off debts is not the way.

230020.explanation: We invest in equal opportunities and extra support for children in poverty; assistance with school costs is part of this.

230021.explanation: Public transport is the responsibility of the transport region/province, so funding must come from there. Additionally, the community bus in Volendam is a private initiative that Zeevang can also take up. The municipality should support such initiatives.

230022.explanation: We involve residents in traffic safety and use tools such as speed display poles.

230023.explanation: We support paid parking in the old town centers for tourists/visitors; residents park free of charge.

230024.explanation: A built parking facility does not have to be free. A small fee can reasonably be charged for a safe, covered spot near the center.

230025.explanation: We want accessibility with a balance between cars, pedestrians, and bicycles, with attention to safety.

230026.explanation: The CDA will continue to push for the realization of the tunnel near Broek in Waterland (N247).

230027.explanation: This is not a municipal task; private initiatives will have to be assessed.

230028.explanation: We must continue working on improving safety on bike paths and investing in cycling routes.

230030.explanation: The CDA has this as a priority; together with partners, we need to explore the need, feasibility, and location for a hospice.

230031.explanation: We support informal caregivers through respite care and assistance.

230033.explanation: Involving young people in politics is very valuable, which can be done with a children's mayor or, for example, the youth council at schools as has been happening for years.

229971.explanation: The opinion of residents is very important, but we believe the municipality already provides sufficient instruments to express this opinion, such as the right to speak at the municipal council. Organizing referenda is financially and organizationally very complex and may even be unfeasible.

229972.explanation: The municipality of Edam-Volendam strives to be a top sports municipality. This requires professional management and maintenance of sports halls.

229973.explanation: Whether a reduction is possible remains to be seen. ONS Belang believes internal municipal organization should be reviewed first before increasing burdens. By smartly managing personnel policy and investing strategically, municipal burdens can decrease, making reductions possible.

229974.explanation: Tourists should also pay for using facilities that cost residents money. For example, tourists also use our roads.

229975.explanation: Taxes must have a clear and specific purpose. The dog tax is not earmarked and benefits general funds without a direct link to facilities for dog owners. Therefore, we are against reintroducing this tax.

229976.explanation: With a large housing shortage, we prefer to give priority to youth, seniors, or single parents with children who need social rental housing.

229977.explanation: Responsibility for integration largely lies at the national level. We believe the municipality should focus on its core tasks and be prudent in expanding expenditures that are already covered by national schemes and funding.

229978.explanation: Too many trees have disappeared in recent years, leading to an increasingly bare municipality. Trees play an important role in keeping our environment livable: they provide cooling during heat periods and absorb water, thereby helping to mitigate flooding.

229979.explanation: The municipality must prevent visual pollution on the horizon as much as possible.

229980.explanation: As long as there is space in primary schools, it is good to use school buildings efficiently.

229981.explanation: Coffeeshops can lead to drug tourism and related nuisance for the neighborhood. This can negatively affect livability and public order within the municipality.

229982.explanation: Vandalism costs the municipality a lot of money. Additionally, people traveling by public transport should feel safer at bus stops located outside built-up areas.

229983.explanation: The use of a baton by municipal enforcement officers does not fit their current tasks and responsibilities. Enforcement should primarily focus on prevention, de-escalation, and visible presence. For situations requiring heavier means, the police are responsible.

229984.explanation: First-time buyers face many problems financing their first home. This way, they get the desired support.

229985.explanation: ONS Belang prefers more affordable homes for purchase instead of social rental housing. We consider the nationally mandated 30% sufficient.

229986.explanation: This is necessary in a municipality where space for new construction is limited. ONS Belang does not want unchecked growth of tall apartment complexes and advocates strict criteria that locations must meet for high-rise buildings.

229987.explanation: Slumlords have also become an increasing problem in our municipality. In times of housing scarcity, a measure like this is effective to give especially first-time buyers a fair chance at owning a home.

229988.explanation: We are all eagerly awaiting the Lange Weeren. This new construction project outside the built-up area is urgently needed to tackle the housing crisis. So, we disagree.

229990.explanation: ONS Belang finds this too easy. What is the reason for the low income? We prefer to retain the freedom to provide customized assistance rather than help everyone. Don’t feel like working and therefore have little money? Then ONS Belang will not help you.

229991.explanation: ONS absolutely supports a 30 km/h speed limit there. ONS Belang wants a lively town center with sports and education, which naturally involves many children in the built-up area. Traffic should be adjusted accordingly. We are fine with 30 km/h in the built-up area.

229992.explanation: This is one of the basic necessities. If families or women cannot afford this, the municipality must take responsibility.

229993.explanation: We encounter too many cases where residents/entrepreneurs are passed from pillar to post, and/or do not feel heard or taken seriously. An independent ombudsman could be a step in the right direction.

229994.explanation: One of ONS Belang’s main points. No new municipal office on the Tase site. This location is perfect for the realization of starter and senior apartments. This was also a campaign promise from all parties. ONS Belang still supports this plan.

229995.explanation: One of the biggest annoyances for residents is no longer being able to speak to a civil servant. Especially seniors struggle with the transition to artificial intelligence. ONS Belang chooses human contact. By phone or email.

229997.explanation: Absolutely. ONS Belang has never understood why approximately 500 square meters had to be removed because it was too busy on the dike. De Beun can come back immediately.

229998.explanation: It is important that alongside existing companies that relocate, new businesses are given space to realize their dreams on the new business park. ONS Belang prefers companies from our own municipality rather than from outside on the new park.

229999.explanation: At this time, we see no reason to restrict or impose limitations on the tourism sector.

230000.explanation: This is not a task for the municipality but should be left to parties/companies with the necessary expertise.

230001.explanation: There are subsidies available from the national government for this.

230002.explanation: As far as there are signals of exploitation of migrant workers in our municipality, the municipality must actively counter this.

230003.explanation: Designating dog walking areas provides clarity for dog owners and prevents unnecessary nuisance and irritation.

230004.explanation: This measure should not be an end in itself but a means to keep waste collection costs (and thus waste taxes) as low as possible. Many other measures are also conceivable.

230005.explanation: Many companies in our municipality are involved in developing sustainable products or want to make their operations more sustainable. These companies should be encouraged to grow, including on a new business park in the Purmer.

230006.explanation: Farmers are crucial for our food chain. Therefore, the farmers within our municipality must be given the space and opportunity to use the available agricultural land, which is becoming increasingly scarce, for this purpose.

230007.explanation: Replacing tiles with greenery should be encouraged, but not with tax money.

230008.explanation: Public spaces have an important role when it comes to sports, exercise, and social interaction. The municipality should do as much as possible for this. But that does not necessarily mean there must be a park where residents can skate and rollerblade.

230009.explanation: The Volendam museum is important for the municipality. New initiatives, such as plans for a Cats museum, can also count on our support.

230010.explanation: Sports and culture connect our community, are good for children’s development, and combat loneliness. That is why it is important that everyone can participate. We offer youth and elderly who cannot afford it a (financial) helping hand.

230011.explanation: Safety is important, but we should not overdo it with measures. The specific nuisance experienced especially in Volendam’s center calls for tailored solutions instead of preventive frisking.

230012.explanation: Safety is important, and especially the safety and sense of security of women require attention. Offering free self-defense courses can improve women’s sense of safety.

230013.explanation: Not only digital fraud but also phone scams and the online dangers that mainly young people face are a big problem. The local government can do more to prevent these dangers.

230014.explanation: If the opportunity arises to develop an area into a housing location, the municipality must do everything possible to realize this. Buying land itself should always be an option, according to ONS.

230015.explanation: Building with sustainable materials such as wood is not an end in itself for ONS, but a means to build faster and more cheaply. To encourage this use, we support the position that 20% of all new homes should be built with sustainable materials.

230016.explanation: A mix of social rental homes (for youth and seniors) and larger owner-occupied homes (for families) ensures faster movement between these households, depending on one’s life phase and financial capacity.

230017.explanation: Insulating a home is costly and therefore not feasible for everyone. Especially for homes with the poorest insulation, the savings on energy costs are greatest. That is why the local government should financially support these measures.

230018.explanation: The municipality helps people with disabilities find a job. This is necessary and important. There are enough ways to assist without costing money.

230019.explanation: This is not a core task of the municipality. Moreover, this is a financial risk the municipality cannot bear.

230020.explanation: Children have the right to equal treatment and education. The municipality can play a role in providing the basic needs for a good start in education.

230021.explanation: Accessibility of facilities is important for all residents of the municipality, also in areas where no regular public transport runs. A community bus is a proven means for this.

230022.explanation: The dynamic speed signs are fun gadgets but add little to the actual, lasting reduction of driving speed.

230023.explanation: We are in favor of free parking, especially for residents of the municipality of Edam-Volendam. Tourists should not park in the center in our view. Other regulatory measures must be taken for this.

230024.explanation: Free parking in a parking garage is not a realistic starting point. A parking garage is expensive to build and maintain. This must be paid from parking fees. Otherwise, the taxpayer would have to pay, which is not an option for ONS.

230025.explanation: The design of the centers of both Edam and Volendam should focus on safe movement for pedestrians and cyclists. However, accessibility for both residents and entrepreneurs by car remains a principle in our vision.

230026.explanation: Broek in Waterland is one of the bottlenecks in regional traffic flow in Edam-Volendam and Waterland. The tunnel will bring improvement here. Together with the redesign of the intersection at the Schouw, this could mean a significant improvement in the accessibility of the municipality.

230027.explanation: The center of Volendam is visited on foot. If people want transportation between Marinapark and Noordeinde, they can use bus line 316.

230028.explanation: Separate bicycle paths provide the safest situation for cyclists. This is a priority for ONS. An important caveat is that this layout requires a lot of space, which is often not available.

230030.explanation: Too often, people in their final phase of life must rely on locations outside the municipal boundaries. While in this phase, the importance of proximity and security is great. ONS supports the establishment of a hospice within Edam-Volendam. Better today than tomorrow.

230031.explanation: Informal care is a crucial form of care. With the upcoming aging population, this importance only increases. It is a municipal task to provide support for the care of the elderly within our municipality.

230033.explanation: A fantastic initiative to get children enthusiastic about (municipal) politics. Moreover, it forms a good way to give the voice of children a place in political decision-making.

229971.explanation: The VVD believes in representative democracy; council members are elected to make decisions.

229972.explanation: Fits with the 'participation society' and fewer government tasks.

229973.explanation: Core goal of the VVD: keep housing costs for local residents as low as possible and maintain facilities. After the property tax was increased by about 50% last year, it must be different now!

229974.explanation: Because of the enormous excessive increases in recent years, it is time to normalize taxes. And much of it goes into the general fund instead of being used for tourism.

229975.explanation: Unnecessary extra tax burden on a specific group, while that money disappears into the general fund.

229976.explanation: Fair distribution; local residents who have been waiting for years must not be passed over.

229977.explanation: The focus should be on own initiative and work, not just on subsidies.

229978.explanation: Especially for the municipality itself. Preservation of a green and livable environment for future generations.

229979.explanation: First cover roofs; agricultural land is scarce and valuable for food and nature.

229980.explanation: Practical for parents and efficient use of social property.

229981.explanation: Protection of public health and the village character.

229982.explanation: Prevention of vandalism and increase of the feeling of safety.

229983.explanation: Boa officers must be able to protect themselves against aggression during their work.

229984.explanation: Essential to give own youth a chance in the tight housing market. Costs the municipality little through a revolving fund.

229985.explanation: Too high; hampers feasibility of developments and construction of transition and owner-occupied homes.

229986.explanation: Preservation of the village view is paramount, unless in very specific locations.

229987.explanation: In the current situation, it must be prevented that speculators and investors disrupt the already overheated market and drive prices even further up.

229988.explanation: To solve the housing shortage, building on edges and in village ribbons is sometimes necessary.

229990.explanation: Assistance must be temporary and aimed at activation, not permanent dependency.

229991.explanation: Main roads must remain thoroughfares (50 km/h), 30 km/h only near schools and shopping streets. In ribbon villages such as Warder, Middelie, and Etersheim, decisions about speed limits or other safety measures are made in consultation with village councils.

229992.explanation: Not a core task of the municipality; personal responsibility.

229993.explanation: Too bureaucratic and expensive; existing complaint procedures should suffice.

229994.explanation: The VVD wants to critically examine costs and does not want residents to pay extra for this.

229995.explanation: Innovation that improves service delivery and saves costs.

229997.explanation: Business-friendly and contributes to the carnival tradition.

229998.explanation: Stimulating local entrepreneurship and economic growth is good, but redevelopment of the Julianaweg industrial area must also take place.

229999.explanation: Balance between livability for residents and income from tourism.

230000.explanation: Support innovative solutions to counter network congestion to connect new homes and businesses. Provide spatial planning assistance (which also costs money), and financing of batteries is the responsibility of the neighborhood itself.

230001.explanation: The VVD prefers continuation of tax benefits or national schemes rather than local subsidies.

230002.explanation: Enforcement of decent housing and prevention of exploitation is a must.

230003.explanation: Reduces nuisance elsewhere and increases the well-being of pets.

230004.explanation: The polluter pays; stimulates waste separation.

230005.explanation: Encourages entrepreneurs to innovate and build future-proof.

230006.explanation: Provided this happens through fair market forces and not as 'free money'.

230007.explanation: Residents have personal responsibility to maintain or green their gardens. This does help against urban 'heat stress'.

230008.explanation: Dependent on budget availability and the needs of the youth.

230009.explanation: Culture is important, but museums should also try to generate their own revenue.

230010.explanation: The VVD is cautious about 'free' passes; prefers targeted support. Work must pay off.

230011.explanation: Safety of party-goers and entrepreneurs comes above all else.

230012.explanation: Not a municipal task; the private market can provide this.

230013.explanation: There is a lot of national attention through campaigns. Education is an effective and relatively inexpensive method of prevention.

230014.explanation: This way the municipality retains control over affordability and speed of construction.

230015.explanation: Contributes to climate goals without paralyzing all construction.

230016.explanation: Promotes social cohesion and prevents 'disadvantaged neighborhoods.' Regulations also require this.

230017.explanation: This already happens. It structurally lowers energy costs, which is better than extra allowances every year.

230018.explanation: Entrepreneurs who practice corporate social responsibility may be supported.

230019.explanation: Undermines personal responsibility and encourages moral misconduct. And consider who would pay for this...

230020.explanation: Children should never be the victims of their parents' financial situation.

230021.explanation: Essential for accessibility and livability of smaller communities.

230022.explanation: Positively influences driving behavior without immediate fines.

230023.explanation: Definitely for residents. Indispensable for the local economy and attractiveness of the villages.

230024.explanation: This would cost local taxpayers a lot of money while in surrounding municipalities parking in garages is paid everywhere.

230025.explanation: Entrepreneurs and residents must be able to reach their destinations without obstacles.

230026.explanation: The only real solution for years of traffic jams. Funded by subsidies from the national government, Transport Region, and Province.

230027.explanation: VVD supports a water shuttle service. This must be financed by the market (entrepreneurs), not by taxpayers.

230028.explanation: Significantly increases safety of vulnerable road users.

230030.explanation: The municipality must facilitate through zoning plans and possibly provide land.

230031.explanation: Relief for informal caregivers prevents them from becoming overburdened themselves (which is more expensive).

230033.explanation: A good way to involve youth early in local politics.

229971.explanation: BVNL considers influence of our residents on important decisions very important.

229972.explanation: BVNL finds it important that our residents can exercise influence on their living environment.

229973.explanation: BVNL advocates for the lowest possible burdens for our residents. That requires realism: less revenue from property tax means other financial choices must be made.

229974.explanation: Our tourist tax is below the national average, while we belong to the top 5 municipalities with the most tourists. Tourists all use our facilities. BVNL believes they may pay more for this.

229975.explanation: BVNL sees that the dog tax has only been used for general funds; dog tax payers never directly benefited. Also, enforcement is expensive. No cat tax is levied, so why a dog tax?

229976.explanation: BVNL believes our own residents should never be disadvantaged by refugees with residence permits. They must queue at the end like everyone else.

229977.explanation: BVNL believes that responsibility lies with the newcomers themselves. We want them to stop holding out their hand. They must invest in a future in the Netherlands. If they do not want to invest in us, they should go back.

229978.explanation: As far as BVNL is concerned, even more trees should be planted. We lag far behind other municipalities in green living environment. More greenery is extremely important for our health, well-being, and enjoyment of life.

229979.explanation: BVNL believes solar panels belong on rooftops. Agricultural land is for food production. A country that cannot feed itself is doomed.

229980.explanation: BVNL finds it unnecessary to compulsorily concentrate education, childcare, and after-school activities on one site. This can create a false sense of security and limits children's ability to learn to deal with different environments and people outside school and childcare.

229981.explanation: BVNL believes it should be taken out of illegality. Also, it must be controllable, like alcohol. Cannabis also has many medicinal benefits for many people, for example via cannabis oil. Alcohol is much more dangerous, also as a gateway to hard drugs.

229982.explanation: BVNL wants to use this means only (temporarily) in places where it is necessary to ensure safety or to detect crime.

229983.explanation: BVNL believes a baton can greatly help with the feeling of safety, control, and de-escalation in conflicts. Obviously, enforcers must be very well trained in its use to prevent escalation caused by themselves. Several successful trials have been held in the Netherlands.

229984.explanation: BVNL thinks it will only have a price-raising effect on owner-occupied homes. Furthermore, the municipality should not act as a loan bank. The municipality also urgently needs this money for other important matters for our residents.

229985.explanation: BVNL is actually a strong supporter of more (affordable) owner-occupied homes. This strengthens our own identity and the independence of buyers.

229986.explanation: BVNL believes this comes at the expense of our village characters.

229987.explanation: BVNL believes everyone should have the opportunity to own their own home.

229988.explanation: BVNL wants more housing construction for our residents - preferably exclusively for our residents - including outside the built-up area. Otherwise, new construction in the Lange Weeren would not be possible either.

229990.explanation: BVNL feels that an enormous part of the annual municipal budget already goes to social provisions, which are at a considerable level. We want to see people with low incomes make maximum efforts themselves to improve their situation. Of course, we will help anyone who truly cannot try.

229991.explanation: But BVNL does consider it necessary that tailored safety measures are arranged in specific locations. We have previously called attention to this through an approved motion.

229992.explanation: BVNL believes our social provisions are sufficient for people to take care of themselves. BVNL also believes that such measures only increase dependency on the local government. That is never good for people.

229993.explanation: BVNL believes the normal complaints procedure should be sufficient. However, we too often hear signals that residents feel unheard or even mistreated. In such cases, an ombudsman offers at least a safety net for them.

229994.explanation: BVNL believes there is a plan in which objections and remarks have been well considered. 19 out of 20 potential locations proved unsuitable. Combining the current municipal sites at the Tase site also creates multiple locations where housing can be developed. A win-win situation.

229995.explanation: BVNL believes it must add value for the resident, for example by enabling them to get a response to their question more simply and quickly.

229997.explanation: BVNL believes the beun (beer tent) should return as soon as possible, of course only if it can be done safely. The beun belongs to the Volendam fair!

229998.explanation: BVNL adheres to the consideration that the new site is intended for the relocation of existing companies from Julianaweg. If there is leftover space, there is of course room for start-ups.

229999.explanation: BVNL believes the market is perfectly capable of regulating this itself. If there is still a need for extra regulation, we will simply arrange that.

230000.explanation: BVNL would like our residents to be supported in feeding energy back into the grid. We also want initiatives explored to store and reuse heat.

230001.explanation: BVNL believes this initiative should lie with the residents themselves. We do not want climate coercion or financial steering from the municipality. We want the municipality to focus on affordable, proven, and voluntary sustainability solutions.

230002.explanation: BVNL believes there are already more than enough legal options for the police to do so.

230003.explanation: BVNL believes they should be fenced off. This is to ensure safety.

230004.explanation: BVNL believes tariff differentiation leads to waste tourism, pollution, and thus more costs. It has been successfully implemented almost nowhere in the Netherlands. Diftar punishes good behavior and leads to illegal dumping. The municipality should first get bulky waste, commercial waste costs, and enforcement in order.

230005.explanation: BVNL finds the term "sustainable" often unclear and often an empty slogan. How do you verify that? We want equal opportunities for all entrepreneurs and expect them to always care about the environment.

230006.explanation: BVNL believes farmers are already more than capable of taking care of this, with or without existing subsidies from the province and/or agricultural collectives. BVNL does appreciate that they continue to be engaged in this.

230007.explanation: BVNL believes these costs are low enough that everyone who cares about nature can take care of it themselves. The municipality can play a facilitating role by collecting removed tiles and giving them another purpose.

230008.explanation: BVNL thinks any form of sport is enormously good for our residents, especially for the youth.

230009.explanation: BVNL knows that subsidy schemes and contributions in forms such as housing already exist. It is up to museums to remain socially relevant. Then they can also obtain private funds. That mix of private and public money is normal everywhere in the Netherlands.

230010.explanation: BVNL believes our social provisions are sufficient for people to take care of themselves. BVNL also believes that such measures only increase dependency on the local government. That is never good for people.

230011.explanation: BVNL believes we must do everything possible to promote the safety of our residents, especially our children.

230012.explanation: BVNL believes we must prevent a case like Lisa from Abcoude from occurring in our municipality. Being able to physically defend yourself against attackers is extremely important for everyone - and especially for women.

230013.explanation: BVNL believes vulnerable groups like the elderly should be helped. BVNL also wants their environment to be sufficiently facilitated to assist them.

230014.explanation: BVNL wants our municipality to retain control and provide direction itself. If the municipality had owned the land for Lange Weeren itself, building would have started long ago. Moreover, this is the cheapest building solution for our residents.

230015.explanation: BVNL knows that Dutch homes already have to comply with strict legal sustainability requirements. Imposing even more sustainability requirements means more obstacles to building. BVNL wants to build more, especially for our youth and elderly.

230016.explanation: BVNL believes we must prevent segregation or the concentration of social problems. Unfortunately, we know examples of this in our municipality. BVNL also believes that offenders causing nuisance must be tackled firmly.

230017.explanation: BVNL considers that the responsibility of landlords. Moreover, the municipality does a lot via the Fixbrigade.

230018.explanation: BVNL knows this already happens through participation in various Social Return initiatives. Also, wage cost subsidy is already possible for people unable to work at full wage value.

230019.explanation: BVNL believes people can already rely on municipal debt counseling. Help is always available in an hopeless situation. But help can also be requested long before the situation becomes hopeless. But you always remain personally responsible!

230020.explanation: BVNL believes our social provisions are sufficient for people to take care of themselves. BVNL also believes that such measures only increase dependency on the local government. That is never good for people. Additionally, schools also have arrangements for this.

230021.explanation: BVNL finds the connection of Zeevang - preserving the village characters - very important for our residents.

230022.explanation: BVNL considers (traffic) safety very important. Research shows that electronic speed signs are the most effective way to raise driver awareness and change behavior. BVNL also believes people who continue to misbehave despite this should be tackled firmly.

230023.explanation: BVNL believes parking in the centers of Edam and Volendam should remain accessible and easy for residents. At locations with structural parking pressure, such as Oude Kommen, targeted and smart solutions are needed to fairly distribute available space and limit nuisance, without burdening residents.

230024.explanation: Free parking in the garage on Julianaweg encourages use and reduces parking pressure in surrounding neighborhoods. If this proves financially unfeasible, complementary policies must prevent tourists and FC visitors from resorting to residential streets.

230025.explanation: BVNL considers good accessibility of the center important for residents, inhabitants, and businesses. Additionally, we believe that better cycling connections and facilities should greatly improve accessibility for cyclists. This will also encourage many people to leave their cars home.

230026.explanation: BVNL knows this has been requested more than once and is a strong supporter. It provides permanently good flow towards Amsterdam. This is very important for commuters from our municipality and our companies.

230027.explanation: This is an idea from BVNL! It can be implemented in multiple ways: as a tourist attraction but also as a real shuttle service to bring tourists who park their car or bus at Marinapark to the center. By the way, we also consider a ferry service a good idea for the same reasons.

230028.explanation: BVNL finds (traffic) safety for vulnerable road users extremely important. Dedicated cycle paths contribute greatly to that safety. Moreover, it can encourage more people to leave their car at home and is a boost for tourism if people can cycle comfortably here.

230030.explanation: BVNL is naturally a strong supporter of good care for people in their final phase of life. A hospice can play a very important role in this by providing care and the opportunity to remain in a familiar environment.
