This also makes it easier for family and friends during a difficult period.

230031.explanation: BVNL considers informal caregivers indispensable to society. Those in need of help then see familiar faces, and the healthcare system is not overburdened. BVNL believes that informal caregivers themselves should not be overburdened either. By the way, assistance is available at the Neighborhood Support Center for this purpose!

230033.explanation: BVNL believes it should be inherent to the municipality to hear the voice of children. This can be done in various ways, for example via the annual youth council. BVNL always participates because we find it important that children are heard and become politically aware.

229971.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes that residents should have the opportunity to express their opinion through a referendum on major changes within the municipality.

229972.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam supports local authority but opposes the shifting of municipal responsibilities.

229973.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes it must stop with carrying out prestige projects that are financed by repeatedly increasing the property tax (ozb).

229974.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam is of the opinion that the tourist tax is justified as adequate facilities exist for tourists.

229975.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes that dog tax is only justified when the municipality also provides adequate facilities for walking dogs.

229976.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes that social rental housing should be distributed fairly. Priority for status holders increases pressure on the housing market and is unjustifiable for young people, starters, and seniors who have been waiting for years.

229977.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes integration is important, but the municipality should not have to spend more money on it. We believe this responsibility belongs to the national government. Therefore, we think no additional money should be spent on integration.

229978.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam stands for the preservation of greenery but believes replanting must be done with tailored solutions. Not every tree cutting requires one-to-one replacement, but careful consideration and compensation where possible are necessary.

229979.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes the municipality should not decide what a landowner may do with their land. At the same time, we want to protect the open landscape and consider large-scale solar fields on agricultural land undesirable.

229980.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes this choice belongs to the parents of children and not to local politics.

229981.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam wants to protect livability and safety in our villages and is therefore against the establishment of coffee shops within the municipality.

229982.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam is cautious about camera surveillance. Safety is important, but structural problems cannot be solved by cameras alone. Prevention and presence on the streets (including private security) work better.

229983.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes the safety of enforcement officers (boa's) is paramount. Their equipment must be appropriate for their tasks and situation. This requires tailor-made solutions, not standard armament.

229984.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes the municipality should assist residents buying their first home with a starter loan to increase their chances on the housing market.

229985.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes housing development should match local needs. This requires a tailored approach per location, with space for social rental, affordable purchase, and homes for starters and movers.

229986.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam opposes large-scale high-rise buildings. Housing must fit the village character and scale of our municipality, not urban high-rise buildings over six floors.

229987.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam wants to keep homes available for residents. A mandatory owner-occupancy rule prevents investments and speculation and increases opportunities for starters and families.

229988.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes housing development should align with local needs. If local demand requires housing that is not possible within existing village boundaries, new construction outside the built-up area should be allowed.

229990.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes residents who are financially struggling should receive support. Aid should target people who really need it and contribute to self-reliance, not prolonged dependency.

229991.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes traffic safety is paramount. A general 30 km/h speed limit is not necessary everywhere. Each street should be assessed to determine what is safe and logical for all road users.

229992.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam wants to tackle period poverty specifically. Assistance should reach residents who struggle financially, for example through existing minimum income schemes or social organizations. A free universal provision for everyone is unnecessary and not targeted.

229993.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes complaints must be taken seriously. This can be done by improving and making existing complaint procedures more accessible, without immediately creating a new role or administrative layer.

229994.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes plans for a new city office on the former Tase site should be stopped. We believe the current locations should be renovated, and the former Tase site should be used for housing development.

229995.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam is open to smart digital tools but values personal contact. AI can assist with simple questions as long as residents can always reach a staff member.

229997.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam stands for preserving traditions. The traveling show (beun) is part of the fair and contributes to atmosphere, unity, and local entrepreneurs within clear and safe agreements.

229998.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam wants to provide space for starting entrepreneurs but opposes fixed percentages. Tailored approaches per business park ensure a healthy mix of companies.

229999.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes tours should not cause nuisance for residents. Rules can help where necessary, but fixed maximums for all situations go too far. Tailored solutions and good agreements work better.

230000.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam is open to new sustainable solutions but believes major investments are wise only when technology and costs are proven. The municipality must manage tax money carefully.

230001.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes sustainability must be feasible and fair. General subsidies from the municipality are not the solution; support should target those who truly need it.

230002.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes exploitation within and beyond municipal boundaries is unacceptable. Everyone working here should be paid and treated fairly and be able to live and work safely. Enforcement of this is necessary.

230003.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes facilities for dog owners can be improved. More designated off-leash areas contribute to livability and prevent nuisance.

230004.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam is strongly opposed to paying per container. This system leads in practice to more littering and ultimately higher cleaning costs for municipalities.

230005.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes sustainability is important but does not want automatic priority for sustainable companies.

230006.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes farmers deserve appreciation for their role in the landscape. However, we support targeted assistance in nature management, not general subsidy schemes without result obligations.

230007.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes greening is important but favors informing and stimulating rather than general subsidies. Awareness and practical support work better.

230008.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam is open to new sports and play facilities, but these investments must align with local needs and available space.

230009.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam values culture and heritage. Support for museums is possible but must be targeted and fit within the municipality's financial capabilities.

230010.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes that every resident should be able to participate in society.

230011.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam values safety, but searches should only be used in cases of concrete risks and not as a standard measure. Freedom and proportionality must always be taken into account.

230012.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam considers safety important for everyone. Prevention and resilience can be part of policy, but not exclusively focused on one group. Safety requires a broad approach.

230013.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes residents should be digitally resilient. Targeted education, for example in schools, helps prevent online fraud and cybercrime.

230014.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes affordable housing is necessary but is cautious about active land policy. The municipality should only purchase land if it is financially responsible and truly contributes to affordability.

230015.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam values sustainability, but does not want to impose fixed percentages that make building more expensive. Sustainable materials should be encouraged as long as they remain affordable.

230016.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes mixed neighborhoods can contribute to livability, but does not want to impose obligations. The composition of neighborhoods should fit the local situation and needs.

230017.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes energy costs must be lowered for residents. Targeted support for home insulation helps reduce housing and energy expenses. Additionally, saving energy contributes to environmental improvement.

230018.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam stands for an inclusive labor market. Support can be appropriate for social enterprises, such as the Botterwerf, which sustainably guide people distant from the labor market. General subsidies to companies are not self-evident.

230019.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes residents with debts should be helped. However, paying off debts is not a structural solution. Good debt counseling, guidance, and prevention are preferable.

230020.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes schools and school boards are primarily responsible for equal opportunities. The municipality should provide a safety net for families in serious financial trouble so that no child is excluded, but should not pay consistently for all school supplies.

230021.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam finds accessibility in Zeevang important. A neighborhood bus can improve connections between villages and help residents who are less mobile.

230022.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes traffic safety is important. Speed signs can help, but only in places where they demonstrably contribute to safer traffic.

230023.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes the centers must remain accessible and attractive. Free parking supports local entrepreneurs and prevents residents and visitors from being discouraged.

230024.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes parking should remain affordable and accessible. A free parking garage on Julianaweg can relieve pressure on the center and improve accessibility.

230025.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes the center of Volendam must remain accessible and attractive. Good accessibility supports local entrepreneurs and prevents residents and visitors from being discouraged to visit the center of Volendam.

230028.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes safety for cyclists is important. Separate bike paths can contribute to safety, but only in places where it is necessary and spatially possible.

230031.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes caregivers are indispensable. Support and respite care must be available, but flexible and appropriate to the situation, not fixed in a rigid monthly obligation.

230033.explanation: Local Edam-Volendam believes it is important to involve children in their living environment. However, this is better done through schools, youth participation, and concrete projects, rather than an extra symbolic role.

statements.title: During the fair, the municipality should again permit the beun (the covered terrace).
statements.moreInfo: The fair in Volendam always had a "beun" as a covered terrace for visitors. That beun was not safe enough. For that reason the municipality has not granted permission for the beun in recent years.
statements.pro: Some parties believe that "de beun" is a tradition at the Volendam fair.
statements.con: Other parties believe the makeshift workshop is, according to experts, unsafe. Safety comes first.

statements.title: The municipality should spend more money on integrating newcomers into society.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld uitgeven aan

titleParts.glossary: Newcomers learn the language and customs of the Netherlands.
People who are new to the Netherlands.

statements.moreInfo: The municipality is responsible for the integration of newcomers. Newcomers are people who have not lived in the Netherlands for long, for example refugees with a residence permit. They must learn the Dutch language and about Dutch society. A municipality may determine how that is done. In Edam‑Volendam every newcomer receives personal advice about a suitable school or course.
statements.pro: Some parties believe that good integration creates a strong community. Well-integrated newcomers find work faster, which is good for the local economy. Newcomers should be able to fully participate in society, and good integration helps with that.
statements.con: Other parties point out the municipality already funds associations that provide language lessons and guide newcomers toward work. Newcomers must invest in their own future in the Netherlands and are responsible for their own integration. The national government should provide more money for newcomers’ integration; that is not the municipality’s task.

statements.title: Edam-Volendam should have its own ombudsman for complaints about the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: An independent person who helps residents with complaints about the government.

titleParts.text: krijgen voor klachten over de gemeente.

statements.moreInfo: An ombudsman or ombudswoman is independent and helps citizens who run into problems with the government. The municipality of Edam‑Volendam does not have its own ombudsman. Residents with complaints about the municipality can go to the National Ombudsman.
statements.pro: Some parties believe many residents have problems with the municipality. An ombudsman can properly examine their complaints. Many residents feel unheard, and an ombudsman can listen to them. Having its own ombudsman can improve the municipality’s service.
statements.con: Other parties think having its own ombudsman is too intrusive. The municipality’s normal complaints procedure is good enough; a separate ombudsman is unnecessary. Appointing one would cost too much money.

statements.title: Low-income residents should receive a pass from the municipality that allows them to participate in sports and cultural activities for free.
statements.moreInfo: Single people who earn less than €1,600 net per month fall below the poverty line. For a couple with two children under 13 that amount is €2,625. In Edam‑Volendam 1.3% of residents live below the poverty line. A survey shows 12% of households in Edam‑Volendam have difficulty making ends meet. With a municipal pass people with little money can exercise for free or visit a museum.
statements.pro: Some parties believe that participation in sport and culture creates contact and helps fight loneliness. People living in poverty should be able to participate fully in society.
statements.con: Other parties believe there are already sufficient arrangements for people with little money. They argue such schemes make people more dependent on the government.

statements.title: The municipality should stop the plans for a new town hall on the former Tase site in Volendam.
statements.moreInfo: A new town hall will be built on the former Tase site in Volendam. The city council decided that last December. The site formerly housed the Don Bosco college and a Tase furniture showroom. The site has been empty for years. In addition to the town hall there will be a parking garage and 62 social rental homes for seniors and starters.
statements.pro: Some parties believe there is a housing shortage. Therefore the municipality should only allow homes to be built on the former Tase site. The municipality has no money to build a new town hall. Building a new town hall is not important now.
statements.con: Other parties think the other locations for a new town hall are unsuitable. Civil servants currently work spread across four old municipal buildings and deserve a good workplace. There will also be housing on the former Tase site, which is a good combination of working and living.

statements.title: The municipality should invest in neighborhood batteries to store electricity from solar panels.
statements.moreInfo: A neighbourhood battery is a small unit in which energy is stored. Households with solar panels in the area are connected to it.
statements.pro: Some parties believe the power grids are full. Neighborhood batteries reduce pressure on the grid. The municipality should help households switch to clean energy, such as solar power.
statements.con: Other parties believe the municipality should not fund the power supply for private households. It is still unclear whether the neighborhood battery technology works well.

statements.title: The police should be allowed to carry out preventive searches in Volendam's entertainment/nightlife area.

titleParts.glossary: Searching someone's pockets and clothes to check for weapons and drugs.

statements.moreInfo: Frisking is the searching of someone's clothes, pockets or bag. The police do this to check people for weapons. Normally the police may only frisk if there is suspicion someone has committed an offense. With preventive frisking there does not need to be suspicion; it is done as a precaution. The municipality may temporarily designate certain areas where the police may frisk people preventively.
statements.pro: Some parties believe that preventive searches increase safety in Volendam’s nightlife area. More and more people come to Volendam to go out, so the chance of problems is greater and control is important.
statements.con: Other parties think Volendam’s entertainment area is not that dangerous. Preventive searches are unnecessary. The municipality of Edam-Volendam should not go too far with measures.

statements.title: There should be a shuttle service between the Marinapark holiday park and Noordeinde in Volendam.
statements.moreInfo: It is about a 15‑minute walk from Marinapark holiday park to Noordeinde in Volendam. There is no public transport.
statements.pro: Some parties believe day-trippers and tourists can park at Marinapark and take the shuttle to Volendam. This reduces traffic in the center.
statements.con: Other parties believe setting up a shuttle service is not a task for the municipality.

statements.title: The municipality should introduce a system where residents pay per bin for their waste.
statements.moreInfo: In the municipality of Edam‑Volendam every household pays a general waste collection charge. The municipality can also introduce a system where residents pay per container they put out. A container for residual waste would then be more expensive than one for organic waste (GFT).
statements.pro: Some parties believe that if residents pay per container, they pay less when they separate their waste well. This is fair because the polluter pays. In Edam-Volendam waste is poorly separated, which costs a lot of money. Paying per container could be a good solution.
statements.con: Other parties argue businesses produce most of the waste and should pay more taxes. If residents must pay per container, they will be more likely to dump waste illegally.

statements.title: The municipality should continue to block the opening of coffeeshops.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.moreInfo: A coffeeshop is a shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed). The municipality of Edam‑Volendam currently has no coffeeshop.
statements.pro: Some parties believe residents should be able to buy soft drugs in a safe place. Cannabis can also help with medical complaints. Alcohol is much more dangerous to health than soft drugs.
statements.con: Other parties think using soft drugs is unhealthy. A coffeeshop can attract visitors from outside the municipality, causing nuisance. Edam-Volendam is a village municipality and a coffeeshop does not fit there.

statements.title: Edam-Volendam should allow residential apartment buildings with more than six floors.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality can set rules for how tall new buildings in the municipality may be. This can vary by location. Those rules are in the zoning plan.
statements.pro: Some parties believe housing shortage is severe. Taller apartment blocks with more units are needed.
statements.con: Other parties believe higher residential towers do not fit the village character of the municipality.

statements.title: The municipality should stop giving refugees with residence permits priority for social rental housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.

titleParts.text: geen voorrang meer geven op een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.moreInfo: Refugees with a residence permit are people who applied for asylum and are allowed to stay in the Netherlands; they are status holders. Municipalities are obliged to help status holders find housing. The national government determines every six months how many status holders a municipality must house. This number is based on the municipality's population. In 2026 Edam‑Volendam must house 92 status holders. Municipalities may decide who gets priority when allocating social rental homes; status holders do not automatically get priority. It depends on the municipality's rules. In Edam‑Volendam refugees with a residence permit do get priority for a social rental home.
statements.pro: Some parties believe many residents have been waiting for years for a social rental home in Edam-Volendam and should not be disadvantaged. Refugees with a residence permit should therefore also be on a waiting list.
statements.con: Other parties think refugees with a residence permit also deserve housing and a place in society.

statements.title: At least 40% of newly built homes in Edam-Volendam should be social rental housing. In Edam-Volendam, 30% of new homes are currently social rentals.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Edam-Volendam moet minimaal 40% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Edam-Volendam is nu 30% van de nieuwe woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.moreInfo: For a social rental home you pay lower rent. They are usually owned by a housing corporation. Only people with a lower income can get such a home. Many people want such a home. The waiting list is long.
statements.pro: Some parties believe there are too few social rental homes in the municipality. In recent years mainly expensive rental and owner-occupied homes have been built, creating an imbalance between social housing and expensive homes.
statements.con: Other parties say building new homes costs a lot of money and more than 30% social rental housing is financially not feasible. The municipality should build more affordable owner-occupied homes instead of social rentals.

statements.title: The municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who police safety in Edam-Volendam should be issued batons.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: ) die in Edam-Volendam controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.moreInfo: A boa is a special enforcement officer (municipal enforcement officer). In many municipalities boas enforce order and safety. They can, for example, issue fines for illegal parking or cycling where prohibited. Recently boas have been allowed to carry force tools, such as batons or pepper spray. The municipality decides about that.
statements.pro: Some parties believe municipal enforcement officers (BOA’s) must be able to defend themselves. In other municipalities enforcement officers have batons, and this works well. Enforcement officers should clearly show they have authority; a baton helps with that.
statements.con: Other parties believe weapons provoke violence. Municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) are not properly trained to use weapons. Using weapons is a police task; BOAs should not take over police duties.

statements.title: Companies that operate sustainably should be given priority when locating on new business parks.

titleParts.glossary: Something you can (re)use for a long time.

titleParts.text: werken moeten voorrang krijgen om zich te

titleParts.glossary: To build, to place somewhere (a building, organization or household).

statements.moreInfo: A new business park is coming to the Purmer. The municipality can set requirements for companies that want to locate there.
statements.pro: Some parties believe this stimulates sustainable entrepreneurship and creates a green economy in Edam-Volendam.
statements.con: Other parties think the municipality should not favor anyone; every entrepreneur should have the same opportunities.

statements.title: The municipality should install more signs that alert drivers to their speed.
statements.moreInfo: Speed signs are digital boards that show faces (smileys) to indicate how fast cars are driving. A red face means a car is driving too fast. A green smiling face means the car is not speeding.
statements.pro: Some parties believe this is good for road safety. These kinds of signs make drivers more aware of their driving behavior.
statements.con: Other parties believe such signs do not help prevent speeding and that even more signs create a cluttered streetscape.

statements.title: The municipality should pay off the debts of people who cannot resolve them themselves.
statements.moreInfo: One in 20 households in the municipality of Edam-Volendam has a problematic debt. Municipalities may take over debts from people who cannot resolve them themselves. Those people must then pay a monthly amount to the municipality. If the debt is not repaid after three years, they no longer have to pay the remaining amount.
statements.pro: Some parties believe the municipality must care for people who need help; that is a municipal task. People regain an overview if the municipality takes over their debts, allowing them to rebuild their lives.
statements.con: Other parties argue people are personally responsible; if they go into debt they must resolve it themselves. There are already sufficient arrangements for people with debts.

statements.title: The municipality should invest in a park where residents can skateboard and roller-skate.
statements.moreInfo: Skating and rollerblading are urban sports. People do these sports outdoors, on streets, in parks or on squares. For example on the site behind the Singel in Edam, where there is a skatepark and fields for playing football or basketball.
statements.pro: Some parties believe that all forms of sport are good, especially for young people.
statements.con: Other parties believe that there are already enough places where young people can play sports.

statements.title: Property tax should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.moreInfo: Property tax (OZB) is the annual tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property. The amount depends on the WOZ value: the estimated market value of the building or land on January 1 of the previous year. The municipality decides how high the OZB is.
statements.pro: Some parties believe that the property tax should remain as low as possible.
statements.con: Other parties believe that the municipality should cut spending elsewhere if property tax is lowered.

statements.title: The municipality of Edam-Volendam should have a children's mayor.
statements.moreInfo: A children's mayor is a child who represents all children in the municipality for one school year. Primary schools usually nominate a child from group 7 or 8. A children's mayor contributes ideas about child-friendly municipal plans and occasionally speaks with the real mayor.
statements.pro: Some parties believe it is important to bring municipal government closer to young people. A children’s mayor is a good way to involve children in local politics at an early age.
statements.con: Other parties believe that a children's mayor is a symbolic position. The municipality should consistently listen to children's opinions. There are better ways for the municipal government to hear children's views, for example through schools.

statements.title: The municipality should oppose new development outside the built-up area.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet nieuwbouw buiten de

titleParts.glossary: An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.moreInfo: New housing outside the built-up area means that homes are built outside the villages, for example in meadows or rural areas.
statements.pro: Some parties believe that the landscape in the municipality of Edam-Volendam must be preserved.
statements.con: Other parties believe that the housing shortage is severe. Therefore the municipality should allow housing construction, even in rural areas.

statements.title: The municipality should distribute tampons and sanitary pads for free.
statements.moreInfo: Research shows that one in eight women in the Netherlands cannot afford tampons and sanitary pads. Municipalities can provide menstrual products for free via, for example, the food bank or special distribution points such as the town hall, the library, community centers, schools or sports clubs.
statements.pro: Some parties believe the municipality should help women who cannot afford tampons and sanitary pads. This way the municipality can discreetly assist women who lack the money for these products.
statements.con: Other parties believe there are sufficient measures for people with little money. Women are responsible for buying their own tampons and sanitary pads.

statements.title: The residents of Edam-Volendam should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Edam-Volendam moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.moreInfo: In a referendum the municipality poses a single-issue question to voters. They can vote for or against. In a municipal referendum all residents of that municipality who are 18 or older may vote. In the Netherlands the result of a referendum is not binding; the municipality can make a different decision.
statements.pro: Some parties believe a referendum is a good way to let residents participate in important municipal decisions. A referendum increases residents’ involvement in local politics.
statements.con: Other parties believe residents can already express their opinions in other ways, for example via the right to speak at meetings. Organizing a referendum is complex and expensive.

statements.title: The municipality should give low-income residents money to insulate their homes.
statements.moreInfo: More than half a million Dutch households have difficulty paying their energy bills. It is estimated that around 400 households in Edam-Volendam cannot or struggle to pay their energy bill.
statements.pro: Some parties believe more people will insulate their homes, which lowers their energy bills. It is good for the environment if more people insulate their homes.
statements.con: Other parties believe homeowners are responsible for insulating their own homes. That also applies to landlords who rent out properties. There are already enough programs to help people insulate their homes in the municipality of Edam-Volendam.

statements.title: The municipality should ensure neighborhoods have a mix of social rental and owner-occupied housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet zorgen voor wijken met

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.moreInfo: A mixed neighborhood is an area where people with low incomes and people with higher incomes live together. The municipality can aim for such a mix in new housing developments.
statements.pro: Some parties believe a mixed neighborhood prevents people from living apart. Mixed neighborhoods stop problems from accumulating in one area, preventing the emergence of a disadvantaged neighborhood.
statements.con: Other parties believe a mixed neighborhood should not be mandatory. The municipality should assess housing needs per neighborhood.

statements.title: Farmers should receive money from the municipality to improve nature.
statements.moreInfo: Municipalities may pay farmers for nature management. They can, for example, manage field margins and ditches or protect meadow birds.
statements.pro: Some parties believe preserving nature ensures a pleasant living environment in the municipality of Edam-Volendam. Farmers are good stewards of nature, so the municipality should pay farmers for managing nature.
statements.con: Other parties believe farmers already receive money from the province for nature management. Additional municipal funding is not needed. Farmers should be free to decide what to do with their land.

statements.title: The municipality should ensure that informal caregivers can get three days of help or replacement each month.

titleParts.glossary: Someone who provides unpaid and often long-term care for sick family members or friends.

titleParts.text: elke maand drie dagen hulp of vervanging kunnen krijgen.

statements.moreInfo: Informal carers are people who care for a loved one who needs help. They can be partners, family members, friends or neighbors. In the Netherlands there are about 5 million informal carers.
statements.pro: Some parties believe informal caregivers are indispensable because they help prevent the healthcare system from becoming overloaded. A few days of rest per month help caregivers keep going. Caring for others is demanding, and the municipality should support caregivers.
statements.con: Other parties believe the municipality is not the employer of informal caregivers. There are already sufficient measures to relieve caregivers.

statements.title: Children from low-income families should receive money from the municipality to buy school supplies.
statements.moreInfo: Municipalities can help low-income families with arrangements for school supplies for children, such as notebooks, pens, a planner and a schoolbag.
statements.pro: Some parties believe it is important that all children have equal opportunities. Children should never suffer because their parents have no money. The municipality must prevent children from being bullied because they don’t have certain belongings.
statements.con: Other parties believe there are already sufficient programs for children from low-income families. These kinds of programs make people too dependent on the government. Schools should ensure all children receive school supplies; that is not the municipality's task.

statements.title: Parking in the future garage on Julianaweg in Volendam should be free.
statements.moreInfo: A parking garage will be built on the former Tase site on Julianaweg in Volendam. The municipal council decided this last December. The site formerly housed the Don Bosco College and a Tase furniture showroom and has been empty for years. In addition to a parking garage, there will be the city office and 62 social rental homes for seniors and starters.
statements.pro: Some parties believe free parking in the new garage on Julianaweg will mean fewer people park in residential neighborhoods and in the center of Volendam.
statements.con: Other parties believe building a new covered parking garage costs money. People who park there should pay for it.

statements.title: The municipality should introduce stricter rules for guided tours for tourists, such as a maximum number of people per group.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality of Edam-Volendam attracts about 3.8 million visitors annually. Most visit Volendam, about 3 million. Edam receives 1.1 million visitors. Zeevang sees more than 90,000 people.
statements.pro: Some parties believe large groups of tourists cause nuisance in Edam and Volendam. The municipality must ensure a better balance between living and earning money from tourism. Strict rules are needed to keep the centers of Edam and Volendam livable.
statements.con: Other parties believe entrepreneurs can limit the nuisance themselves. The municipality does not regulate tourist guided tours. Limiting the number of tourists goes too far. There is no reason to introduce stricter rules for tourist tours.
